commit.message:
Drive sold-out row color from a CSS variable

The .dark .bg-emerald-50 override apparently wasn't winning the cascade
even inside @layer utilities. Drop the Tailwind color class entirely on
the sold-out table row and drive the background from --soldout-bg /
--soldout-bg-hover CSS variables defined per theme. This removes any
dependence on utility specificity.

commit.message:
Fix PWA navigation: use Link instead of <a> for admin/portal links

Plain <a href> tags cause full page reloads in standalone PWA mode,
which resets to start_url. Switched admin link in portal sidebar and
portal links in admin layout to Next.js Link for client-side nav.

Also added visible loading spinner in messages thread list while
fetching from eBay API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: magnetic snap, multiple openings, legend toggle in room builder

Three interaction upgrades bundled together:

MAGNETIC SNAP
- All vertex, anchor, and endpoint handles snap magnetically to nearby
  compatible points (20px screen-pixel radius)
- dragExcludeRef prevents self-snap; snap targets refresh every render
- Concentric blue ring glow renders on the snap target while dragging
- Works on touch/pointer across desktop, iPad, and phone
- Snapping and opening detection coexist: snapped points close gaps,
  separated points produce openings

MULTIPLE OPENINGS
- computeOpenings() detects every gap >0.5" between consecutive segment
  endpoints (including wrap-around last→first)
- Each opening renders its own dashed indicator line + dimension pill
- Multiple openings numbered ("Opening 1: 36\"", "Opening 2: 24\"")
- Labels update live as walls and anchors are dragged
- isClosed() updated to use segStart() for anchor-aware detection

LEGEND TOGGLE
- "Hide legend / Legend" button added to canvas toolbar (next to zoom)
- showLegend state in main page; passed as prop to PerimeterCanvas
- Legend SVG group renders conditionally — full canvas visible when hidden

commit.message:
Fix dark-mode utility overrides by placing them in @layer utilities

Plain unlayered rules in globals.css were being beaten by Tailwind's
generated .bg-emerald-50 etc. Wrapping the overrides in @layer utilities
puts them in the same cascade layer, where the 0,2,0 specificity of
".dark .bg-emerald-50" reliably wins over the 0,1,0 single-class
utilities. Also add an explicit dark variant on the sold-out row in
course-sessions-manager as a belt-and-braces backup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: git worktree isolation for fixes — merge or discard

Fix with AI now runs in an isolated git worktree:
- Creates branch codevetter/fix-{timestamp} + worktree
- Agent edits files in worktree, not your working directory
- Diff shows worktree changes vs HEAD
- Merge: commits in worktree, merges into main branch, cleans up
- Discard: removes worktree + branch, no traces left
- Falls back to in-place fix if worktree creation fails
- .codevetter-worktrees added to .git/info/exclude automatically

commit.message:
refactor: profiles now use all 5 strategies, not just grid

ProfileRunner completely rewritten:
- Receives same signals from all strategies (Grid+DCA+Momentum+Futures)
- Each profile applies different aggressiveness:
  Conservative: 70% size, 2% stop, 1x leverage, strong signals only
  Moderate: 100% size, 3% stop, 2x leverage, normal signals
  Aggressive: 140% size, 5% stop, 3x leverage, any signal
- Trailing stops per profile with different thresholds
- Fair comparison: same data, different risk appetite

commit.message:
fix: cast new_val to str before assigning into StringDtype columns

pandas 2.x reads CSV with dtype=str as StringDtype which rejects float
assignments. Convert fetched numeric values to str at each .loc assignment
to match the string-store design of the DataFrame.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: eliminar console.log debug referidos

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(purge): drop per-package arg from go clean -cache

go clean -cache does not accept package arguments; the invocation
'go clean -cache github.com/owner/repo/...' exits with status 1.
Drop the per-tool loop and run 'go clean -cache' once with no args,
which is the correct way to clear the Go build cache.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: streaming actually works now — spawn_blocking for fix process

The blocking stdout reader was on the Tauri main thread, preventing
events from being delivered. Now runs in tokio::spawn_blocking so
fix-progress events reach the frontend in real-time.

fix: isolate agent temp files per session + fix PaddlePaddle PIR on Windows

- Use a unique UUID subfolder (/tmp/gl/<session_id>/) per agent_stream call
  so concurrent sessions never share/overwrite temp files
- Clean up the session temp dir in a finally block after streaming ends
- Disable PaddlePaddle PIR (FLAGS_enable_pir_api=0) and MKL-DNN at import
  time in document_layout.py to prevent ConvertPirAttribute2RuntimeAttribute
  errors on Windows with PaddlePaddle 3.x
- Auto-fallback from LayoutDetection to PPStructure if new API fails at
  runtime (not just at import time)
- Improve analisi_layout error message to avoid LLM hallucinating wrong causes

commit.message:
remove hardcoded patterns - only detect-patterns API as source

Deleted hardcoded "Contradiction" pattern with vague "pulling in
two directions" text from analyze-dimension callback. Now calls
/api/detect-patterns on dimension completion instead, which returns
specific behavior/question format. No pattern text is ever created
in frontend code — all patterns come from the API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Feat: Stream LLM responses to the UI + Disable RAG (#13)

* fix: remove conflicting playwright devDependency

Removed standalone `playwright@1.55.1` which conflicted with `@playwright/test@1.58.2`. The `@playwright/test` package already includes the CLI binary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: stream LLM responses to the UI

Switches the chat API from `generateText` (blocking) to `streamText` so tokens appear progressively as they arrive. References are appended via `StreamData` before streaming begins and attached to the message on completion.

- `route.ts`: replace `generateText` with `streamText` + `StreamData`
- `submitMessages.ts`: read Vercel AI data stream line-by-line with `onChunk`/`onComplete` callbacks
- `Chat.tsx`: add empty assistant placeholder on submit, grow content per chunk, finalize with references on complete
- New e2e tests with `page.route()` mock covering text streaming, thinking indicator, references, and input state
- Updated integration test to mock `streamText` with a `readDataStream` helper
- Updated unit tests to use new callback-based `submitMessages` signature

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add RAG_ENABLED toggle to disable retrieval pipeline

Adds a RAG_ENABLED constant in constants.ts (currently false) that gates the retrieval and context injection in the chat route. Integration tests mock it to true to keep the RAG pipeline tested while disabled in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: move thinking indicator into Messages component with pulse animation

Moves "AI is thinking..." into Messages.tsx, rendered in place of the message content when an assistant message has empty content. Transitions automatically to streamed text on the first token. Uses animate-pulse with inherited text color for a skeleton-like effect.

commit.message:
Fix music not starting — await AudioContext resume before scheduling

startMusic() now waits for ctx.resume() to resolve before setting
_nextStepTime and starting the interval, so notes are never scheduled
against a suspended context's clock. _musicTick also skips ahead if
the scheduler falls behind (e.g. after a tab switch).

  renders a Plus icon, causing a double "+" in the UI

commit.message:
Remove ADAPTIVE=1 from BED_MESH_CALIBRATE — GoKlipper doesn't support it

The Kobra 3's GoKlipper (Rinkhals) silently fails when
BED_MESH_CALIBRATE gets an unknown parameter, which was causing the
entire start sequence (level, wipe, etc.) to not execute.
Plain BED_MESH_CALIBRATE works — does a full mesh every time.

- Make TableHeader thead sticky at top 68px with a card background.
- Add globals.css rule that pins the first dashboard h1 (text-2xl font-bold) to the top of the viewport with a gray-50 bleed background.

Force 2 decimal places on all cost inputs

Comprehensive SEO improvements across all pages

- Add canonical URLs (alternates.canonical) to all 25+ public pages
- Standardize domain to oneclickit.ai (non-www) across all OG urls,
  JSON-LD, metadataBase, and redirects (www → non-www)
- Add OpenGraph tags to pricing, enterprise, service-area, cybersecurity,
  and add-ons pages (were missing)
- Add FAQPage JSON-LD schema to contact page (4 FAQ items)
- Add Service JSON-LD schema to cybersecurity page with Secure Care plans
- Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD to cybersecurity page
- Add LocalBusiness + Service JSON-LD to service-area page with LA County
  cities for local SEO
- Fix AggregateRating inconsistency: standardize to 4.9/5 (47 reviews)
  across homepage, reviews, and root layout structured data
- Fix homepage LocalBusiness schema: add street address, proper phone,
  structured areaServed
- Reverse www redirect: now www → non-www (matching canonical domain)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix deduction paycheck cutoff and consolidate to single reimbursement

- Issue date on or after payday now pushes to NEXT paycheck (pay period must close before the check — e.g. issued 4/3 → 4/10, not 4/3)
- Split deductions still use sequential checks starting from the correct one
- Reimbursement is now always ONE check for the full total, scheduled 90 days from issue date (same period-cutoff rule applies)
- Client-side preview mirrors the same logic via clientDeductionPaycheck helper
- Preview layout redesigned: deduction table + separate reimbursement summary row
- Success confirmation matches same layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: detect pseudo-version and dirty builds in selfupdate; expand README

- isValidSemver now rejects Go pseudo-versions (vX.Y.Z-0.YYYYMMDDHHMMSS-COMMIT)
  and +dirty builds; dev builds always apply the latest release
- Add update_test.go covering clean releases, dirty builds, pseudo-versions,
  and valid pre-release tags
- README: document all commands (run, start, install, purge, service
  install/remove), daemon profiles table, lezz run vs lezz start,
  pseudo-version update behaviour, and ADHD MCP rung validation tools

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix mergeSTLFiles to handle ASCII STLs (plate slicing broken)

mergeSTLFiles read triangle count from binary offset 80 on ASCII STLs,
getting garbage numbers (42 billion) and skipping them as "truncated".
Merged file had 0 triangles, PrusaSlicer failed.

Now detects ASCII STLs, parses them, converts to binary triangle data
for merging. Works alongside the bakeTransform ASCII fix.

commit.message:
Fix admin.ephia.de root 404 by redirecting / to /dashboard

Previously the root page redirected to /book, which the middleware
rewrote to /not-found on the admin domain, producing a 404. The admin
domain now redirects / to /dashboard directly; unauthenticated users
are then bounced to /login by the dashboard layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix plate slicing: add ASCII STL support for transform bake

The plate merge was producing 0-triangle files because all STLs were
ASCII format (from OpenSCAD) and bakeTransformToSTL skipped ASCII.
PrusaSlicer fallback passed files without transforms, stacking them.

Added parseSTLAscii() to parse ASCII STL facets into the same format
as binary. Both bakeTransformToSTL and autoOrientSTL now handle ASCII.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: loop 2 — 15 fixes from re-audit across resilience, host, protocol, encryption

Resilience (critical):
- Orchestrator: tokio::spawn per connection (was serial blocking)
- Orchestrator: bulkhead integration (50 OPAQUE, 30 TSS concurrent)
- Health endpoint: configurable bind addr (0.0.0.0 when token set)
- KEK reconstruction: 3-attempt retry with exponential backoff

Host compromise:
- sanitize_environment() added to verifier, risk, audit, kt services
- Ratchet secret array zeroized after create_session in both handlers
- RatchetAction Debug redacts initial_key
- GoogleOAuthConfig client_secret uses Zeroizing<String>
- MILNET_SIEM_ENCRYPTION_KEY added to sanitize list
- Gateway key_pem zeroized after TLS parsing
- OAuthLoginForm password zeroized on Drop

Protocol:
- Puzzle store fail-closed in military mode (was warn-only)
- AAL2 12h + absolute 24h session timeout enforcement in token verification
- PQ-VRF constant-time output comparison

Quantum:
- PQ leader election function using ML-DSA-87 VRF

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign BLOCK_ recovery constants for location realism and body-specific needs

BLOCK_POOL_SWIMMING split into two location-honest blocks:
- BLOCK_POOL_LAP: lap pool only (backstroke first, freestyle, flutter kick with board)
  Breaststroke kick permanently excluded (tibial torsion adductor loading)
- BLOCK_POOL_REC: rec pool only (water walking, lateral shuffles, hip circles,
  high knees, quad pull to chest pull)

BLOCK_FOAM_ROLL_STRETCH updated with tibial torsion compensation chain:
- Added: adductor foam roll, TFL/IT band upper, psoas stretch
- Added: calf roll with foot rotation for posterior tib
- Added: sleeper stretch + cross-body stretch (shoulder posterior capsule)
- Plain-language notes on all new exercises for in-app coach support

POOL_RECOVERY updated to use both new pool blocks + foam roll + shoulder prehab

fix: double-tap rename with zero single-tap delay

Track last-tap timestamp per item. First tap fires immediately (no
deferral). Second tap within 300ms triggers rename. No timer needed —
single tap has zero delay and double-tap still works.
